entities.name: BIOLOGICAL NITRIFICATION INHIBITION
entities.description: The ability of certain plants to inhibit the biological conversion of ammonium to nitrate in the soil

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A cereal grain crop studied for its BNI-enabled varieties

entities.description: The process where nitrogen is lost from the soil system

entities.name: NITROGEN UTILIZATION EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which a plant absorbs and uses nitrogen

entities.name: GREENHOUSE GAS EMISSIONS
entities.description: Gases released into the atmosphere that contribute to the greenhouse effect

entities.name: WATER POLLUTION
entities.description: The contamination of water bodies caused by agricultural runoff

entities.description: The total amount of nitrogen-based fertilizer used in crop production

entities.description: A major sorghum-growing state in India

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the sorghum production study was conducted

entities.name: LIFE CYCLE ASSESSMENT
entities.description: A systematic analysis of the environmental impact of a product throughout its life cycle

entities.name: NITROGEN FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The practice of applying nitrogen fertilizer to crops

entities.name: FARMERS' BENEFIT
entities.description: The economic profit gained by farmers from crop production

entities.name: UREA FERTILIZER SUBSIDIES
entities.description: Government financial assistance provided to reduce the cost of urea fertilizer

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit of land

entities.name: SUSTAINABLE FOOD SYSTEM
entities.description: A food system that provides food security and nutrition while preserving environmental resources
